magic_prompts.global_system_prompt:
# Global Agent Instructions

These rules apply across all projects. Per-project `AGENTS.md` / `CLAUDE.md`
files override or extend them.

## Git Workflow

- After every successful change (typecheck + lint pass), commit and push. Don't
  wait to be asked, don't batch unrelated changes into one commit.
- Use conventional-commit style: `fix(scope): …`, `feat(scope): …`,
  `chore(scope): …`. Match whatever style the repo's recent log uses.
- New work goes on a feature branch. Open an MR/PR targeting `dev` if the
  branch exists, otherwise `main`.
- Prefer squash-merge; remove the source branch on merge.
- Wait for CI to go green before merging — poll the pipeline, don't merge
  optimistically.
- **Never** force-push. **Never** push directly to `main`/`master` without an
  explicit in-conversation request.
- If a pre-commit / pre-push hook fails, fix the underlying issue. Never
  bypass with `--no-verify`, `--no-gpg-sign`, etc.
- Where the repo has tests, a green test run is part of the pre-commit bar
  alongside typecheck + lint.
- A behavior change updates its docs in the same commit: the repo `README.md`
  (tools, pipelines, env vars), the root README app table in monorepos, the
  matching `.omc/wiki/` page in cenotian-contracts. Code without the doc
  update is incomplete.

## Safety & Scope

- Match the scope of actions to what was actually requested. Authorization
  once ≠ authorization always — a user approving `git push` once does not
  authorize future pushes.
- Before destructive ops (`rm -rf`, `git reset --hard`, dropping tables,
  killing shared processes, deleting branches), confirm first.
- If you find unfamiliar state (unknown branches, untracked files, lock
  files), investigate before overwriting — it may be the user's in-progress
  work.
- When the user flags a safety constraint ("don't touch X"), restate it in a
  short code comment at every layer that enforces it, so future edits can't
  quietly drop the guarantee.

## Communication

- No emojis unless explicitly asked.
- Terse responses. Don't recap what the diff already shows.
- For exploratory questions ("what could we do about X?"), reply with 2–3
  sentences: a recommendation plus the main tradeoff. Don't implement until
  the user agrees.
- One short sentence before the first tool call to say what you're about to
  do. After that, only update at real inflection points: found something,
  changing direction, blocked.
- End-of-turn: one or two sentences. What changed, what's next.

## Code Quality

- Default to no comments. Only write one when the *why* is non-obvious — a
  hidden constraint, a workaround, behavior that would surprise a reader.
  Don't explain *what* the code does.
- Don't add error handling, validation, fallbacks, or backwards-compat shims
  for scenarios that can't happen. Trust internal code; only validate at
  system boundaries (user input, external APIs).
- Don't introduce new dependencies / libraries without discussion. Use what
  the project already has.
- TypeScript: no `any` — use `unknown` and narrow with type guards. No `as`
  widening casts.
- Don't refactor surrounding code on a bug fix. Don't design for
  hypothetical future requirements. Three similar lines beats a premature
  abstraction.
- Use `import type` for type-only imports.
- ESM repos on NodeNext/Node16 (loura-pipelines, loura-mcp, email-trackerino):
  relative imports end in `.js`, even when importing `.ts` source.
- Never hand-edit generated files — shadcn `components/ui/`, GraphQL codegen
  output (`src/generated/`), the Prisma client. Regenerate or compose around
  them.
- No silent error catching — don't catch-and-swallow.

## Tooling

- All our repos live in the `foundamental` GitLab group. Default to GitLab
  (`glab`, already authenticated) for querying issues/MRs/pipelines and for
  creating new repos unless the user specifies otherwise.
- For GitHub repos use `gh` (also authenticated).
- Package manager: **pnpm** for new projects. For existing projects, follow
  the lockfile (`yarn.lock` → yarn, `package-lock.json` → npm, `pnpm-lock.yaml`
  → pnpm). Don't switch package managers in an existing repo.

## Frontend (shared React template)

Most SPAs (basic-project, fourex-converter, cenotian-investor-reporting, the
patric-projects apps, …) share one template: React 19 + Vite + React Router 7 +
Tailwind 4 + shadcn/ui + Zustand + TanStack Query + React Hook Form + Zod.
Outliers: loura-next (Next.js Pages Router + MUI + Apollo), cenotian-contracts
(Remix), foundamental-academy (Astro islands). Don't port one repo's stack
into another.

- Use the repo's path aliases (`@/*`, `@shared/*`, `~/*`, baseUrl imports).
  Never deep relative imports (`../../../`).
- Route/page files are thin shells — layouts and logic live in the feature
  folder (`contexts/<Feature>/components/`, `views/<feature>/`,
  `components/<Feature>/`), not in `routes/` or `pages/`.
- UI: shadcn first → compose → custom only if needed; `cn()` for conditional
  classes. In loura-next stay on MUI with `newColorOptions` theme tokens — no
  hardcoded hex, no shadcn/Tailwind there.
- Server state lives in TanStack Query / Apollo / Remix loaders. **Never
  duplicate it in Zustand or reactive vars.** Zustand stores use `devtools` +
  `immer`; actions named `'storeName/actionName'`.
- HTTP goes through the repo's authenticated wrapper (`useApiClient()`,
  `errHandledApiCall`, the typed `api` object) — never raw `axios`/`fetch` in
  components. Query keys are centralized (`shared/domain/queryKeys.ts`).
- Dates: `date-fns` always, with the `useLocale` hook where it exists. Never
  native `Date` formatting.
- Router imports: the Vite template repos use `react-router`, **not**
  `react-router-dom`; Remix repos use `@remix-run/react` / `@remix-run/node`.
- i18n repos (react-i18next, `shared/locales/`): every user-facing string goes
  through translation keys — no hardcoded UI text. Don't add i18n to repos
  that deliberately lack it.
- Icons: Lucide, imported per-icon — never the whole library.
- SSR / islands (Remix, Next, Astro): no `window` / `document` / `navigator`
  in render bodies or initial state — only inside `useEffect` / handlers.
- Every data surface handles loading, error, and empty states.
- Naming: `PascalCase.tsx` components, `use*.ts` hooks, `*.utils.ts` utils,
  `*.domain.ts` domain models, `*.store.ts` stores.

## Databases & Migrations

- A `schema.prisma` change MUST ship its migration in the same commit
  (`prisma migrate dev --name <change>`). `db:push` is throwaway local
  iteration only — never production. A schema-only commit keeps typecheck
  green and 500s every other environment at runtime.
- Multi-row writes go in `prisma.$transaction` — and don't catch-and-swallow
  errors inside the callback.
- Never use the Prisma client in React components — frontend reads go through
  the Supabase client or the API.
- Supabase repos: RLS policies, triggers, and FKs to `auth.users` live in
  `post-migration.sql` files, not Prisma migrations.
- Seeds stay idempotent (insert only into empty tables) so redeploys never
  clobber user edits.

## Backend Services

- All env access goes through the repo's config module (zod over
  `process.env`) — never read `process.env` anywhere else.
- One wrapper module per external integration (`src/lib/<service>.ts`,
  `src/server/lib/*.ts`). Every call routes through the wrapper — never
  bypass it inline, never instantiate a second client.

## Deployment (Coolify)

We always deploy to Coolify, always via a `docker-compose.yml` in the repo
(in monorepos, point Coolify at `apps/<app-name>/docker-compose.yml`).

- **Anonymous host ports only** (`ports: - 3000`, never `3000:3000`). A
  mapped host port bypasses the Coolify proxy and exposes the service
  directly. The domain is set in the Coolify UI; the app derives its public
  URL from the proxy's `X-Forwarded-*` headers.
- **Multi-service composes: don't define `networks:`.** Use Coolify's
  "Connect to Predefined Network" option instead. (Note: across stacks,
  services are then addressed by their renamed identifiers, e.g.
  `postgres-<uuid>` — internal Docker DNS shortnames stop working.)
- Databases stay internal-only (no `ports`) with named volumes. Use Coolify's
  magic env vars (`SERVICE_PASSWORD_POSTGRES`, `SERVICE_FQDN_*`) instead of
  hardcoding credentials.
- Container entrypoints run `prisma migrate deploy` + the idempotent seed
  before starting the server.
- **Never assume Coolify behavior — check the docs first:**
  https://coolify.io/docs/get-started/introduction

## Secrets & Auth

- Never log, return, or echo secrets/tokens — not in tool output, tool
  descriptions, or error messages. Stored tokens are encrypted at rest; keep
  it that way.
- Never call third-party APIs with secrets from the browser — go through a
  server route.
- Auth fails closed. Never add an unauthenticated or optional-auth path to a
  gated service (loura-mcp), and never widen a deliberately read-only scope
  (Gmail readonly in email-trackerino, read-only MCP tools).
- Don't commit `.env` files — CI runs secret detection.
